original_registry_entry.issuing_organisation: National Institute for Health and Care Excellence
original_registry_entry.exact_document_title: Suspected sepsis in people aged 16 or over: recognition, assessment and early management
original_registry_entry.population: People aged 16 years and over with suspected sepsis, excluding people who are or have recently been pregnant.
original_registry_entry.clinical_setting: Community, ambulance, acute hospital, and emergency assessment settings.
original_registry_entry.applicability_note: Exact for suspected sepsis recognition and assessment in non-pregnant people aged 16 years and over; partial for a broad fever or suspected-infection workflow and not directly validated for UAE pathways.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.1–1.2 — recognition, suspected infection, source, and risk factors
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports suspected-infection and fever presentation documentation, source symptoms, risk factors, and recognition that sepsis can present with nonspecific illness.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendation 1.3 — history, observations, circulation, respiration, skin, urine output, and source examination
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports temperature, heart rate, respiratory rate, blood pressure, consciousness, oxygen saturation, skin signs, urine output, fever or rigors, and examination for the suspected infection source.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.5–1.6 — community and acute risk criteria
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports confusion, respiratory compromise, hypotension, tachycardia, oliguria, temperature abnormality, non-blanching or mottled skin, and immunosuppression as assessed risk features.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendation 1.7 — emergency transfer, monitoring, and information
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports emergency transfer or escalation for high-risk presentations and documented monitoring, warning signs, and how to seek care for lower-risk presentations.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.8 and 1.11 — source identification and investigations
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports clinician-selected blood and microbiology testing, urinalysis, chest radiography, and abdominal or pelvic imaging to identify an infection source.

title: Suspected sepsis in people aged 16 or over: recognition, assessment and early management
publisher: National Institute for Health and Care Excellence
population: People aged 16 years and over with suspected sepsis, excluding people who are or have recently been pregnant.
intended_setting: Community, ambulance, acute hospital, and emergency assessment settings.
